nodes.name: Model prediction
nodes.description: ESOL solubility via mat-gram (SMI-TED): direct SMILES or batch model prediction over an attached Graph table

nodes.name: Predict
nodes.description: SMI-TED ESOL property prediction via mat-gram POST /embeddings (wraps local MAX)
instruction: Send JSON body with smiles set to a SMILES string or list of SMILES (e.g. "CCO" or ["CCO", "c1ccccc1"]).
Prefer when the user gives SMILES in the message (single or few molecules) and does not need table-row batch prediction.
Do not use this when an attached table should be scored row-wise — that is solubility_table / Predict table.
Service runs task=esol (property mode) against MAX on this host.
Response includes mode, task, model path, embeddings, and predictions (log10 mol/L).

nodes.name: Predict table
nodes.description: Batch ESOL model prediction over an attached Graph table: score SMILES column via mat-gram, return rows with prediction column
instruction: Requires an attached table in request context (hold=table). Forward message verbatim and pass table from bindings.

ALWAYS prefer this when a table is attached and the user asks to run a prediction / predict / score / model / ESOL / solubility / logS on a SMILES (or smiles) column, or to put model outputs in a new column (e.g. pred, esol).
Keywords that must route here (not SciPy): prediction, predict, pred, model, ESOL, solubility, logS, score SMILES, run on smiles column.
Extracts a SMILES column and POSTs the list to mat-gram; may name the output column from the user message (default esol).
Do not use SciPy/pandas transforms for model prediction on SMILES — that is this string.
Do not use this for Plotly charts or numeric z-score/ratio/normalize math on non-SMILES columns.
Optional prefixes: 'solubility / predict table: …' or 'solubility_table: …'.
Response includes rows enriched with the prediction column, twin_name, and row_count for a new Graph tab.

nodes.name: Screening candidates
nodes.description: ZINC lead-like + PubChem compound databases via SQL twins on this host's Postgres

nodes.name: Query
nodes.description: Natural-language SQL over zinc_leadlike (query_zinc_leadlike) and pubchem_compounds (query_pubchem_compounds)
instruction: Forward the user message verbatim under message (keep any twin/query prefix).

Prefer when the user asks about ZINC or PubChem molecules, lead-like screening, MW/logP/XLogP ranges, ZINC IDs, PubChem CIDs, InChIKeys, formulas, IUPAC names, tranches, TPSA, H-bonds, or SMILES search.
Do not use this for solubility prediction — that is solubility_embeddings.
Optional prefixes: 'query_zinc_leadlike: …', 'query_pubchem_compounds: …', 'zinc / query_zinc_leadlike: …', or 'pubchem / query_pubchem_compounds: …'.

Twins: query_zinc_leadlike (zinc_id, inchikey, tranche_name, smiles_pattern, mwt_min/max, logp_min/max, net_charge, limit) and query_pubchem_compounds (cid, inchikey, molecular_formula, smiles_pattern, iupac_name_pattern, molecular_weight_min/max, xlogp3_min/max, tpsa_min/max, hbond_donor, hbond_acceptor, total_charge, limit). Null unused filters.
Service selects a SQL twin via explicit name or EmbeddingGemma + LanceDB, extracts params with Lisette, and queries Postgres on this host.
JSON response includes twin_name, row_count, rows, and optional excel (base64) when include_excel is true.
If the user explicitly wants a downloadable spreadsheet, set response_format=excel_file instead of json.

nodes.name: Capabilities
nodes.description: Describe matching ZINC/PubChem SQL twins (query_zinc_leadlike, query_pubchem_compounds) and their required parameters (no DB execution)
instruction: Forward the user message verbatim under message.
Prefer when the user asks what screening query params are required or what twins/filters are available for ZINC or PubChem.
Returns matching twin names, descriptions, and required parameters.

nodes.name: Plotly
nodes.description: Plotly charts over an attached Graph table (requires table hold context)

nodes.name: Chart
nodes.description: LangGraph Plotly analyst: draw charts from the attached table rows
instruction: Requires an attached table in request context (hold=table). Forward message verbatim and pass table from bindings.
Prefer when the user asks to plot, chart, scatter, histogram, bar, line, or visualize columns of the attached table.
Optional prefixes: 'plotly: …' or 'plotly / chart: …'.
Response includes plotly figure JSON (data/layout) plus a short response string.

nodes.name: SciPy
nodes.description: SciPy/pandas numeric column transforms over an attached Graph table (not ML/model prediction)

nodes.name: Transform
nodes.description: LangGraph SciPy analyst: derive or normalize numeric columns from the attached table rows (not SMILES model prediction)
instruction: Requires an attached table in request context (hold=table). Forward message verbatim and pass table from bindings.
Prefer when the user asks for numeric/math transforms only: z-score, standardize, normalize, ratio, combine numeric columns, scipy.stats, filter-math.
Do NOT use this for model prediction, solubility, ESOL, logS, or scoring a SMILES/smiles column — that is Model prediction / Predict table (solubility_table).
Do NOT use this when the user says prediction/predict/pred/model on SMILES.
Do not use this for Plotly charts — that is plotly / chart.
Optional prefixes: 'scipy: …' or 'scipy / transform: …'.
Response includes rows (with new columns), twin_name, row_count, columns_added, and a short response string.
